text:
I have enough to assess the proof-quality angle. Key issues confirmed by reading the source.

**Undocumented defeq through Subtype/coercion wrappers** — `BasedPath.lean:381,383` rewrite the goal `a₀ < ⟨s, hs01⟩` / `⟨s, hs01⟩ ≤ 1` (the order on `I`) into real inequalities purely by `change`, relying on `Subtype.lt`/`Subtype.le` and the `(1 : I)` literal coercion unfolding definitionally. Line 383 has no comment at all; line 381's comment states *what* it does, not why no lemma works. Mathlib provides `Subtype.coe_lt_coe` / `Subtype.coe_le_coe` exactly for this. Same undocumented-`change` pattern at `BasedPath.lean:471, 889, 893`.

**Brittle `simp`-unfolding of a tactic-built definition** — `deformTerminal` is defined in tactic mode wrapping a nested `dite` in `ContinuousMap.mk`; the four lemmas `deformTerminal_apply_of_le/_lt_of_le/_lt` and `endpoint_deformTerminal` (`BasedPath.lean:182,189,195,201`) each prove evaluation by `simp [deformTerminal, …]`, i.e. by unfolding that opaque `by`-constructed term and reducing its branch. This is the fragile-terse-proof case the rubric warns about.

TAUCETI-VERDICT-4de55d3f87ae003450f3a9ef

```json
{
  "verdict": "request_changes",
  "summary": "Proofs are generally robust automation, but several goals are closed by `change` across Subtype/coercion definitional equality without justification (one with no comment), and the deformTerminal evaluation lemmas rely on simp-unfolding a tactic-constructed definition.",
  "findings": [
    { "file": "TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroupoid/BasedPath.lean", "line": "383",
      "issue": "`change s ≤ 1` (and `change ((a₀ : I) : ℝ) < s` at line 381) close the `Set.Ioc` membership goal by unfolding the Subtype order on `I` and the `(1 : I)` coercion definitionally; line 383 has no comment, line 381's comment states what not why.",
      "fix": "Replace with an explicit lemma, e.g. `Subtype.coe_le_coe`/`Subtype.coe_lt_coe` (or `unitInterval.le_one`), instead of relying on defeq.",
      "evidence": "Lines 380-384: `change ((a₀ : I) : ℝ) < s` / `change s ≤ 1` against goal `⟨s,hs01⟩ ∈ Set.Ioc a₀ 1`." },
    { "file": "TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroupoid/BasedPath.lean", "line": "471",
      "issue": "Undocumented `change` tactics rest on beta/structure-projection defeq: line 471 (`change Continuous fun ts : I×I ↦ H ts`), 481/510 (`change endpoint (ofPath …) ∈ U`), 889/893 (`change K_fn (t,(0:I)) = (α'.trans L) 0/1`). Comments at 481/510 describe what, not why; 471/889/893 have none.",
      "fix": "Document why the goal cannot be reached by `rw`/`simp only` with the relevant eval lemma, or use such a lemma instead of `change`.",
      "evidence": "Grep hits BasedPath.lean:471,481,510,889,893; `change`/`show` are flagged when used without a why-comment." },
    { "file": "TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroupoid/BasedPath.lean", "line": "182",
      "issue": "`deformTerminal` is defined in tactic mode (`:= by … refine ⟨ContinuousMap.mk (fun t ↦ f t) …⟩` with an internal `let f := dite …`). The eval lemmas at 182/189/195 and endpoint at 201 prove their results by `simp [deformTerminal, …]`, i.e. unfolding that opaque term and reducing its nested `dite`; any reshaping of the definition breaks all four.",
      "fix": "Give `deformTerminal` an explicit named `toFun` term and a single `deformTerminal_apply` unfolding lemma; derive the branch lemmas from it via `Path.extend` rewriting rather than re-unfolding the def each time.",
      "evidence": "Lines 153-176 define deformTerminal via `by`/`refine`; lines 182,189,195,201 each `simp [deformTerminal, …]`." }
  ]
}
```

verdict_obj.summary: Proofs are generally robust automation, but several goals are closed by `change` across Subtype/coercion definitional equality without justification (one with no comment), and the deformTerminal evaluation lemmas rely on simp-unfolding a tactic-constructed definition.

findings.issue: `change s ≤ 1` (and `change ((a₀ : I) : ℝ) < s` at line 381) close the `Set.Ioc` membership goal by unfolding the Subtype order on `I` and the `(1 : I)` coercion definitionally; line 383 has no comment, line 381's comment states what not why.
findings.fix: Replace with an explicit lemma, e.g. `Subtype.coe_le_coe`/`Subtype.coe_lt_coe` (or `unitInterval.le_one`), instead of relying on defeq.
findings.evidence: Lines 380-384: `change ((a₀ : I) : ℝ) < s` / `change s ≤ 1` against goal `⟨s,hs01⟩ ∈ Set.Ioc a₀ 1`.

findings.issue: Undocumented `change` tactics rest on beta/structure-projection defeq: line 471 (`change Continuous fun ts : I×I ↦ H ts`), 481/510 (`change endpoint (ofPath …) ∈ U`), 889/893 (`change K_fn (t,(0:I)) = (α'.trans L) 0/1`). Comments at 481/510 describe what, not why; 471/889/893 have none.
findings.fix: Document why the goal cannot be reached by `rw`/`simp only` with the relevant eval lemma, or use such a lemma instead of `change`.
findings.evidence: Grep hits BasedPath.lean:471,481,510,889,893; `change`/`show` are flagged when used without a why-comment.

findings.issue: `deformTerminal` is defined in tactic mode (`:= by … refine ⟨ContinuousMap.mk (fun t ↦ f t) …⟩` with an internal `let f := dite …`). The eval lemmas at 182/189/195 and endpoint at 201 prove their results by `simp [deformTerminal, …]`, i.e. unfolding that opaque term and reducing its nested `dite`; any reshaping of the definition breaks all four.
findings.fix: Give `deformTerminal` an explicit named `toFun` term and a single `deformTerminal_apply` unfolding lemma; derive the branch lemmas from it via `Path.extend` rewriting rather than re-unfolding the def each time.
findings.evidence: Lines 153-176 define deformTerminal via `by`/`refine`; lines 182,189,195,201 each `simp [deformTerminal, …]`.
